Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.filter

Class RequestIdFilter

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.filter.RequestIdFilter
public class RequestIdFilter extends Object
Per-request id mint-or-honor filter that stashes the id on the servlet request and echoes it on the response.

Designed to be invoked from a @RestStartCall method so the id is available to every downstream component — the call logger, observability layers, and any application code that reads req.getAttribute(RestServerConstants.REQUEST_ID).

Behavior
  • If the incoming request carries an X-Request-Id header and the value matches the configured validator, that value is honored.
  • Otherwise (header absent, blank, or rejected by the validator), a fresh id is minted via the configured supplier (default: UUID.randomUUID()).
  • The chosen id is stashed on the underlying servlet request under the REQUEST_ID attribute key.
  • The chosen id is echoed on the response as X-Request-Id.
Default validator

The default validator is ^[A-Za-z0-9-_]{1,128}$, which accepts UUIDs and the bulk of distributed-tracing id schemes (W3C Trace Context, OpenTelemetry, Datadog, etc.) while rejecting whitespace, control characters, header-injection payloads, and oversized strings. Customize via RequestIdFilter.Builder.validator(Predicate).

Example usage

@Rest public class ApiResource extends BasicRestServlet { private static final RequestIdFilter REQUEST_ID = RequestIdFilter.create().build(); @RestStartCall public void stampRequestId(HttpServletRequest req, HttpServletResponse res) { REQUEST_ID.apply(req, res); } }

See Also:
  • REST Server — Rate-Limiting and Request-Id Propagation
Since:
10.0.0

    • RequestIdFilter

      protected RequestIdFilter(RequestIdFilter.Builder b)
      Constructor.
      Parameters:
      b - The builder configuring this filter. Must not be null.

  • Method Details

    • create

      public static RequestIdFilter.Builder create()
      Creates a new builder.
      Returns:
      A new builder.

    • apply

      public String apply(jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest req, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse res)
      Mints or honors a request id, stashes it on the request, and echoes it on the response.

      Idempotent: if the attribute is already set on the servlet request (for example by a parent filter), the existing value is honored and re-echoed.

      Parameters:
      req - The servlet request. Must not be null.
      res - The servlet response. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The resolved request id. Never null.
